form_name: R2R - Affair Controlling - Cost Collectors and Budget Availability Control

questions.text: What is a cost object within SAP?
options: A document that records liabilities
A system to manage project milestones
Either a cost center or WBS element
A report listing company expenses

questions.text: Which statement is true regarding the codification of WBS?
options: It is randomly assigned
There are no specific rules
Specific rules must be followed
It is based on the employee ID

questions.text: What is required to activate Budget Availability Control for a Cost Center?
options: Defining a Budget Profile with GL account hierarchy and account groups
Assigning a direct link to nature of expense
No specific profile is needed
Using a single Cost Center for all budget controls

questions.text: What does the Budget Release define in the budgeting process?
options: The total amount of money available
The level of budget control availability check
The maximum number of transactions allowed
The number of Cost Centers involved

questions.text: How is budget management handled for Cost Centers?
options: Manually entered into SAP at each fiscal year
Uploaded from Excel with Fiori App “Import Financial Plan Data”
Automatically calculated by SAP based on previous years
No management needed; it operates automatically

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a principle of WBS budget management?
options: WBS has specific start and finish dates
WBS should not be created for budgets
WBS is capped at 50 per Controller
WBS can be created without a Budget Profile

questions.text: Purchase requisitions cannot be created when the budget is over the defined threshold.
options: True
False

questions.text: WBS cost collectors have a specific end date.
options: True
False

questions.text: Controlling is always a part of the approval workflow for purchase requisitions.
options: True
False

questions.text: Budget consumption is determined by the total amount spent, including both actual and committed amounts.
options: True
False
